name: NAVER HyperCLOVA X SEED Think 32B
license: HyperCLOVA X SEED 32B Think Model License Agreement
archDisplay: Dense multimodal (vision-language) transformer with optional extended-thinking mode
note: Open-weights multimodal reasoning model; strong Korean open model, reported ahead of EXAONE 4.0 32B on Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index at launch (44 vs 43; later shown as 17 under recalibrated v4.1 methodology).
tpsNote: No community GGUF/MLX quantization confirmed yet as of research date; custom VLM architecture not yet supported by llama.cpp

results.notes: tau2-Bench Telecom, per Artificial Analysis (article phrases it as '87 percent in an evaluation that assessed agent tool usage capabilities through a simulated telecommunications customer support scenario') [Corrected during full-corpus audit: Fetched the model's own technical report (arXiv 2601.03286, 'HyperCLOVA X 32B Think') three times independently; Table 5 consistently reports Tau2-Airline 58.0, Tau2-Retail 71.6, and Tau2-Telecom 65.1 for this model — not 87. The existing 87 figure traces only to an Ajupress press article paraphrase ('achieved 87 percent in an evaluation that assessed agent tool usage capabilities...simulated tele]
Reported as 'SEED-IMG' (image split of SEED-Bench) in Table 5, English vision-to-text benchmarks.
Section 5.3 text: 'THINK achieves a score of 63.4' on Video-MME.

Reported as 'Terminal-Bench 1.0' in Table 5, agent benchmarks. The paper's separate 'Terminal-Bench Hard' (9.9) was left out — it doesn't cleanly correspond to either terminal-bench or terminal-bench-2 in the taxonomy.

verificationNotes: Confirmed genuinely real (highest-scrutiny record given Dec-2025 release date) — found live HF model card, arXiv paper 2601.03286, Artificial Analysis model page, and independent press/X coverage (Ajupress, Artificial Analysis and Wes Roth X posts) all describing this exact model. 32B params, license name, 128K context, and text/image/video modalities all confirmed verbatim on the HF page. The '87' tau-bench score is confirmed present on the ajupress.com sourceUrl (paraphrased as '87 percent' for an agentic telecom customer-support tool-use eval, i.e. tau2-Bench Telecom). The note's claim of an initial 44 vs EXAONE (43) at launch, later recalibrated to 17 under AA's v4.1 methodology, was independently verified: X posts from Artificial Analysis/Wes Roth at launch cite 44 outperforming EXAONE 4.0 32B, while the current live artificialanalysis.ai page now shows Intelligence Index 17 under v4.1 methodology — both halves of the note check out.
